episodes.name: Project kickoff discussion
episodes.content: We decided to use PostgreSQL as the primary database for the new analytics platform. The team agreed that Maria will lead the backend development and James will handle the data pipeline. The deadline for the MVP is end of Q2 2025.

episodes.name: Architecture review
episodes.content: After reviewing the architecture, we decided to add a Redis caching layer between the API and PostgreSQL to handle the expected read-heavy workload. Maria raised concerns about data consistency with the cache invalidation strategy.

entities.name: Maria
entities.summary: Backend lead for the analytics platform project. Has experience with distributed systems and PostgreSQL.

entities.name: James
entities.summary: Data pipeline engineer. Responsible for ETL processes and data quality.

entities.name: Analytics Platform
entities.summary: New internal analytics platform with PostgreSQL backend, Redis caching, and a REST API. MVP deadline is Q2 2025.

entities.name: PostgreSQL
entities.summary: Primary database chosen for the analytics platform.

entities.name: Redis
entities.summary: Caching layer added between the API and PostgreSQL for read-heavy workloads.

facts.name: leads_backend
facts.fact: Maria leads the backend development of the Analytics Platform.

facts.name: handles_pipeline
facts.fact: James handles the data pipeline for the Analytics Platform.

facts.name: uses
facts.fact: The Analytics Platform uses PostgreSQL as its primary database.

facts.name: uses
facts.fact: The Analytics Platform uses Redis as a caching layer for read-heavy workloads.
